bullets.text: Q3 CR 95.9, recovering from Q2's 97.6 — rate revision infrastructure deployed ~65 adjustments over two quarters; YTD CR tracking toward full-year 96 target after mid-year slip.
Written premium grew 10% in Q3 — strongest quarterly growth since Q1 2005; rate increases driving premium ahead of unit growth as new business lagged ~5% in Agency and Direct auto.
Special $1.00/share dividend declared in October, payable November 29; full-year investment portfolio total return tracking ~6%; portfolio performing despite low yield environment.
Rate revision response fully engaged after Q2 severity confirmation; ~65 rate actions executed; adequacy expected to be restored to target by early Q4; reaction speed acknowledged as too slow.
Hurricane Isaac most significant Q3 catastrophe; hail, floods, winds, and fires elevated property losses; motorcycle exposure above plan from heat and drought conditions through summer.
ASI non-controlling equity arrangement announced during Q3 — deepening home+auto bundling capability through independent agent channel; Snapshot 'Test Drive' 30-day free trial program announced.
Snapshot 'Test Drive' launched — consumers can evaluate UBI program for 30 days without switching current insurance; program removes adoption barrier for unconvinced shoppers.
New business applications lagged ~5% in Agency and Direct auto and ~1% in special lines and Commercial as rate increases reduced competitive position during adjustment period.
Flo awarded Advertising Week 'Walk of Fame' star — first fictional character so honored; Ad Age 'Top Ten Female Ad Icons' designation confirmed; brand equity expanding beyond insurance category.

Commercial Lines annual policy structure slows rate earn-through vs. personal auto; rate adequacy restoration expected slightly later in Commercial; monitoring competitive response closely via conversion rates.
